drawbacks: The headline features are paid
Negotiation keeps a cut of your savings
Requires linking your bank accounts
more_info: The negotiation cut, explained

Bill negotiation charges a percentage of the savings it wins you — you select the rate within their offered range before it proceeds. Real service, real cost; a phone call to your provider performs the same negotiation for a percentage of zero.
Premium's 'pay what you want' floor

Premium pricing is choose-your-price above a floor, which is friendlier than it sounds and still a subscription. The free tier's subscription-finder is the genuinely valuable part — use it as a mirror, act on it yourself.

listings.name: DreamSite Site Checkup
listings.limits: One page per scan; best-practices scan only
listings.realcost: Nothing. It's the studio's front door — scan free, hire them only if you want.
listings.disclosure: DreamSite and Verified Free share an owner. Same rubric, same scrutiny — details under More info.
free_includes: Instant Speed and Security scores for any web address
HTTPS, certificate, and security-header checks read from the real server response
Load time, page weight, and render-blocking script analysis
Cookie safety, mixed content, version leakage, and mobile-readiness checks
No signup, no email gate — and scanned sites aren't stored
drawbacks: Scans the page you give it, not your whole site
A best-practices read of public information — not a penetration test
Heavily bot-protected sites may block any automated scan
It's a design studio's front door — their pitch lives nearby
listings.get_summary: In practice: a plain-English answer to 'is my site slow or insecure?' in seconds — the checklist a professional audit starts with, minus the invoice.
listings.worth: Professional site audits start in the hundreds; this covers the opening checklist free.
smart_moves: Scan before and after any site change — the scores make regressions obvious
Fix the security headers first; most are one-line server settings
Only scan sites you own or have permission to check
Pair it with Google's free PageSpeed Insights when you want deep performance detail
more_info: Who pays for this?

DreamSite is a design and engineering studio; the checkup is its front door. You get a genuinely free diagnostic, they get to demonstrate competence — the honest version of lead generation, with no email wall in between.
What the scan can and can't see

The exact checks — HTTPS, certificate, security headers — read the real server response and are precise. Content checks cover the single page loaded, and it's a best-practices scan of publicly visible information: not a break-in attempt, and not a substitute for a full security audit on high-stakes systems.
Disclosure, in full

DreamSite and Verified Free share an owner. This listing went through the same rubric as the other 58 — which is exactly why its Value score isn't a 10. If that rubric ever bends for the house, the whole site is worthless; it won't.
listings.short: Yes — completely. Paste any web address and get graded Speed and Security scores in seconds: no signup, no card, no email gate, and the site states scanned addresses aren't stored.
listings.catch: The catch is the business model, stated plainly: the scan is a design studio's calling card. The results are real either way — and the absence of an email gate is more than most 'free audit' tools can say.

listings.name: HubSpot CRM
listings.limits: Branding on free tools; caps on automation and sends
listings.realcost: An account, HubSpot branding on customer-facing tools, and a famous upgrade ladder.
listings.short: Yes — the canonical business lead magnet is itself a real product: a permanently free CRM with contact management, deal pipeline, forms, live chat, and email marketing, no card required.
listings.catch: HubSpot gives the CRM away to sell the paid 'hubs' stacked on top — and the ladder gets steep fast. The free tier is genuinely usable forever; it's also the most polished funnel entrance in software.
free_includes: Full contact and company database with up to a million stored contacts
A deal pipeline, tasks, and activity tracking
Forms, live chat, and a meeting scheduler
Email marketing in the low thousands of sends per month, HubSpot-branded
paid_locked: Removing HubSpot branding from emails, chat, and forms
drawbacks: HubSpot branding sits on everything a customer sees
The upgrade pricing climbs steeply once you're embedded
Your customer data lives in their ecosystem — that's the point
listings.get_summary: In practice: a real CRM for a small business — track every contact, deal, and conversation in one place — at the price of wearing their logo.
listings.worth: Entry-level paid CRMs run $15–30 per user per month; this covers a small team's core needs at $0.
smart_moves: Use the free meeting scheduler and forms — they replace two separate paid subscriptions
Export your contacts periodically; deep CRM lock-in is the real long-term price
Price the paid hubs before you're dependent — the jump surprises people
more_info: Why would they give away a CRM?

HubSpot invented modern inbound marketing, and the free CRM is its masterpiece: once your customer data lives there, every growth need maps to one of their paid hubs. You're not the charity case — you're the pipeline. Used knowingly, it's still one of the best free deals in business software.
The branding toll, explained

Free means HubSpot's name on your customer-facing emails, chat widget, forms, and scheduling pages. For a scrappy startup that's fine; for a polished brand it's the itch that converts you. Budget for the moment it starts to matter.
listings.canada: Available in Canada. Free tier identical. Paid plans billed in USD.

listings.name: Mailchimp
listings.realcost: A free tier cut so thin it's now a demo: 250 contacts, 500 sends a month.
listings.short: Barely, anymore. The famous free plan was cut again in early 2026 to 250 contacts and 500 sends a month with a 250-a-day cap — down from 2,000 contacts a few years ago — with no scheduling, no multi-step automation, and Mailchimp branding on every email.
listings.catch: This is the textbook shrinking free tier: repeated cuts since the Intuit acquisition, and even unsubscribed contacts count against your 250. What was once the default free newsletter tool is now an evaluation sandbox with a famous name.
free_includes: A drag-and-drop email builder and basic templates
Up to 250 contacts and 500 sends a month (250/day)
Basic signup forms and reporting
drawbacks: Limits shrank roughly 96% from the plan's heyday
Unsubscribed contacts still count toward your 250
Can't send twice a month to even a modest full list
listings.get_summary: In practice: enough to test the editor and email a tiny list — a club, not a business. Anything growing outgrows this in weeks.
listings.worth: Paid email tools start around $10–15/month — and several rivals' free tiers now beat this one outright.
smart_moves: Archive unsubscribed and stale contacts — they count against your 250
Compare free tiers before committing: several competitors offer multiples of these limits
If you stay, watch the annual limit changes; this plan has been cut four times
more_info: How the free plan shrank, year by year

Before 2022: 2,000 contacts and 10,000 sends. Then 500 contacts. Then 1,000 sends. Then, in early 2026, 250 contacts and 500 sends with a daily cap. Each cut arrived by email to a user base that had built on the old numbers — a case study in why 'free forever' needs a date stamp.
The unsubscribed-contacts rule

Mailchimp counts subscribed, non-subscribed, and unsubscribed contacts identically against your limit. People who opted out years ago still occupy slots until you manually archive them. On a 250-contact ceiling, that housekeeping is the difference between free and blocked.

free_alternatives.name: HubSpot CRM
free_alternatives.why: Email marketing with far more generous free limits, plus a full CRM

sentiment.praised: Marketers still rate the platform's ease of use and templates well — it's the reason people started here.
sentiment.complained: The complaint pattern is the shrinking free tier: reviewers describe repeated cuts (2,000 contacts, then 500, now 250) and sudden bills when a list crosses the line. The word that recurs is 'bait'.
sentiment.takeaway: The negative reviews aren't about the software failing — they're about the free plan shrinking underneath people who built on it. That's a different kind of failure.

listings.name: Trello
listings.realcost: An account. Ten boards covers most small teams indefinitely.
listings.short: Yes — Trello's free plan is a real permanent tier: unlimited cards and members, ten boards per workspace, and the drag-and-drop kanban that made the product famous, no card anywhere.
listings.catch: The ten-board ceiling is the fence, and it's generously placed — many teams live inside it for years. The squeeze arrives with scale: more boards, bigger file attachments, and admin controls are the paid tier's territory.
free_includes: Unlimited cards, lists, and members
10 boards per workspace
Checklists, due dates, labels, and attachments
Automation in modest monthly doses
paid_locked: Admin controls and advanced views (timeline, calendar, dashboard)
drawbacks: 10-board cap forces consolidation or workspaces gymnastics
Advanced views locked
Owned by Atlassian — expect steady nudges toward its paid family
listings.get_summary: In practice: a household, a small team, or a couple of projects run comfortably free forever. It's one of the fairest freemium fences in software.
listings.worth: Comparable project tools charge $5–10 per user per month for what most small teams do here free.
smart_moves: One board per project, not per week — the 10-board cap rewards tidy structure
Archive finished boards instead of deleting; archived boards don't count against you
The free automation allowance covers recurring-task busywork — set it up once
more_info: Where the fence actually sits

Unlimited cards and people mean the free tier scales with team size, not usage — unusual and generous. The fence is board count and file size. If you're bumping the board cap, merging projects into one board with labeled lists usually buys another year of free.
The Atlassian context
Trello sits inside Atlassian's suite, and the free tier partly exists to feed Jira and friends. Nothing sinister — just know the product's job is to be your gateway, and enjoy the gateway.

listings.name: Calendly
listings.limits: One active event type on free
listings.realcost: An account, and a one-meeting-type ceiling built to chafe.
listings.short: Sort of. Free Calendly genuinely works — unlimited bookings, calendar sync, the polished booking page — but you get exactly one active event type. The moment you need '15-minute intro' AND '60-minute consult,' you've met the paywall.
listings.catch: One event type is a masterfully placed squeeze: enough for a freelancer with a single offer, one short of almost any real service menu. The product is excellent; the fence is the business model.
free_includes: Unlimited meetings booked through your link
One active event type
Calendar connection to prevent double-booking
Automatic time-zone handling and reminders

listings.worth: Paid scheduling starts around $10–12/month; the free tier replaces email ping-pong for one offer.
smart_moves: Make your one event type flexible: one '30-minute call' with an agenda question beats three locked types
Rotate the active type when your needs change — you can swap which one is live
Compare before paying: several rivals' free tiers allow multiple event types
more_info: Why one event type?

It's the cleanest conversion lever in SaaS: the free tier demonstrates the whole product perfectly, and the very first sign of business growth — a second service — is the paywall. Respect the craft; plan around it.
The swap trick
You can deactivate one event type and activate another at will — one active, not one ever. Seasonal businesses ride this lever for years without paying.

listings.name: Loom
listings.realcost: An account, a 5-minute clock, and a 25-video shelf.
listings.short: Sort of. Free Loom records your screen and camera with one click and hosts the share link — but recordings cap at five minutes and your library holds 25 videos before old ones must go.
listings.catch: Five minutes is the squeeze that teaches concision; twenty-five videos is the shelf that fills. Both limits are placed exactly where a habit becomes dependence — which is, of course, the design.

Instant share links, viewable without an account
Up to 25 videos in your library
5 minutes per recording

drawbacks: The 5-minute wall cuts explanations mid-thought
The 25-video cap forces regular deleting

listings.get_summary: In practice: quick how-to clips and feedback videos work beautifully free; anything resembling a course or archive hits both walls fast.
listings.worth: Paid screen recorders and Loom's own tier run $8–15/month; free covers the casual habit.
smart_moves: Script the first sentence before recording — 5 minutes is enough when you don't ramble
Download videos you care about; the 25-slot shelf shouldn't be your only copy
For long recordings, free OBS has no time cap — Loom's convenience is what's metered, not the capability
more_info: Why the limits sit exactly there

Five minutes covers a quick answer but not a demo; twenty-five videos covers a month of habit but not a library. The free tier is engineered to make Loom your reflex, then charge for the reflex. Knowing that, use it for what it's genuinely great at — fast, disposable clips.
The OBS escape valve

Everything Loom meters — length, storage, ownership — is unmetered in free OBS Studio, at the cost of convenience: you host and share the file yourself. Loom sells the frictionlessness; the recording itself was never the scarce part.

listings.name: Google Business Profile
listings.realcost: Nothing in money. Your business data enriches Google's map of commerce.
listings.short: Yes — the single highest-leverage free thing a local business can do online: your listing on Google Search and Maps, with photos, hours, posts, messaging, and review replies, entirely free.
listings.catch: Google gives this away because your business data makes Search and Maps better, and better maps sell more ads. You're both the beneficiary and the content. Claim it anyway — unclaimed profiles get filled in by strangers.

Photos, hours, services, and posts
Replying to reviews and customer messaging
Basic insights on how people find you
drawbacks: Reviews happen to you whether you participate or not
Google's rules govern your own listing
Data flows into the ad ecosystem, and 'Promote' buttons are everywhere
listings.get_summary: In practice: for a local business, this listing often outperforms the website itself — it's where customers actually find hours, directions, and trust signals.
listings.worth: Local-SEO agencies charge hundreds to set up and manage exactly this. The tool itself has always been free.
smart_moves: Claim your profile before a competitor or stranger edits it — unclaimed listings are editable by the public
Reply to every review, especially bad ones; replies are marketing read by hundreds
Post photos monthly — profiles with fresh photos get measurably more clicks
Ignore paid 'Google listing services' cold-calling you; the real thing is free
more_info: The scam industry around a free product

An entire cold-calling industry sells 'Google listing management' to businesses that don't know the profile is free. Some threaten 'suspension' for non-payment. Google charges nothing; anyone demanding payment for the listing itself is the trap this page exists to flag.
Why Google wants this free

Every accurate business profile makes Maps more useful, keeps users inside Google, and creates inventory for local ads. Your free listing is genuinely valuable to you AND a brick in their wall. Both things are true; take the value.

listings.name: PageSpeed Insights
listings.realcost: Nothing. Google wants faster websites; this is them helping you build one.
listings.short: Yes — paste any URL and get Google's own performance diagnosis free: lab scores, real-user Core Web Vitals data, and a prioritized fix list. No account, no limit, no catch.
listings.catch: The catch is comprehension, not cost: the report speaks fluent web-developer. But the scores are the same signals Google's ranking systems care about, straight from the source.
free_includes: Performance scores for mobile and desktop
Real-user Core Web Vitals where Google has the data
A ranked list of specific fixes with estimated savings
Unlimited scans, no signup
drawbacks: The report assumes technical vocabulary
Lab scores vary run to run — trends matter more than single numbers
It measures speed, not security or design
listings.get_summary: In practice: the authoritative answer to 'is my site slow, and why' — the same measurements Google itself uses, handed over free.
listings.worth: Performance audits are a paid consulting line item; the raw diagnosis is free here.
smart_moves: Test mobile first — that's where scores collapse and where Google looks
Run it three times and average; single runs wobble
Hand the fix list to whoever built your site — it's literally their to-do list
Pair with a security-focused scan for the full picture; speed is only half
more_info: Lab data vs. field data

The score comes from a simulated ('lab') load; the Core Web Vitals section, when present, comes from real Chrome users on your actual site. Field data is the truth; lab data is the diagnosis. A site can pass one and fail the other — read both.
Why Google gives away its own audit

Slow sites lose users, and users lost to slowness are users lost to Google's ecosystem and advertisers. Free performance tooling is enlightened self-interest — which is exactly why it's trustworthy: their incentive and yours point the same direction.

listings.name: Qualys SSL Labs
listings.limits: One domain per scan; a few minutes per test
listings.realcost: Nothing. It's a security company's calling card — the honest kind.
listings.short: Yes — the industry-standard free test of a website's HTTPS setup: paste a domain, wait a couple of minutes, get a letter grade with a full breakdown of certificate, protocol, and configuration issues.
listings.catch: It's the same species as the DreamSite checkup: a security firm demonstrating competence by giving the diagnosis away. The scan is slow and deeply technical — and the letter grade is the lingua franca of TLS quality.
free_includes: A full TLS/SSL configuration analysis with a letter grade
Certificate, protocol, and cipher detail
Known-vulnerability checks
No account, no fee
drawbacks: Takes a few minutes per scan
Deeply technical output past the letter grade

listings.get_summary: In practice: the definitive free answer to 'is our HTTPS actually configured right' — the grade your IT provider should be able to explain.
listings.worth: This exact analysis inside a paid security assessment bills at consulting rates; here it's the free sample.
smart_moves: Tick 'Do not show results on the boards' before scanning if the domain is sensitive
Anything below an A is a to-do for whoever runs your server
Re-test after certificate renewals — that's when configurations quietly regress
more_info: Why Qualys gives this away

SSL Labs made Qualys the name enterprises think of for security posture — a lead magnet so good it became the industry's shared measuring stick. The free tool IS the marketing budget, spent on being useful.
Reading the grade honestly
The grade measures your HTTPS configuration, not your overall security. An A+ site can still have a hacked contact form. Treat it as one gauge on the dashboard — necessary, nowhere near sufficient.

listings.name: QR Code Generator (qr-code-generator.com)
listings.limits: Dynamic codes die when the 14-day trial ends
listings.realcost: Your printed materials. Trial codes deactivate and redirect customers to their upgrade page.
listings.short: Beware. The 'free' flow steers you into dynamic QR codes on a 14-day trial — and per the company's own support pages, when the trial ends those codes deactivate and scans land on a service page instead of your content. Menus, flyers, and signs already printed become their advertising.
listings.catch: No card is taken and nothing auto-bills — the hostage isn't your wallet, it's your print run. Static codes made there do keep working forever; the trap is that the interface nudges you toward dynamic, and most users can't tell the difference until customers start scanning dead codes.
free_includes: Static QR codes, which genuinely work forever
A 14-day trial of dynamic (editable, trackable) codes
paid_locked: Dynamic codes that keep working past day 14
Scan analytics and editing after the trial
drawbacks: Trial dynamic codes deactivate and redirect to their service page
Static vs. dynamic is invisible in the printed code — failures surprise you
The 'free' framing sells a countdown as a product
listings.get_summary: In practice: the free part that's safe (static codes) is buried under the free part that expires (dynamic codes) — a design that converts confusion into upgrades.
listings.worth: A dead QR code on a thousand printed menus costs a reprint; the 'free' here can be the most expensive option on this site.
smart_moves: For anything printed, use STATIC codes — from any generator, they work forever
Never print a dynamic QR code created during a free trial, anywhere, ever
Need editable/trackable codes? Pay from day one or don't use dynamic at all
Test-scan every code the week after your trial would end — before your customers do
more_info: Their own docs, quoted honestly

The company's support pages are transparent: dynamic trial codes 'are deactivated and point to a service page rather than the original destination,' while static codes 'continue working.' No deception in the fine print — the trap is that nobody printing a menu reads QR support docs. The verdict is for the design, not the disclosure.
Static vs. dynamic in one breath

Static codes bake your link permanently into the pattern — no service needed, immortal, uneditable. Dynamic codes point to the provider's short link so you can edit the destination — powerful, and alive only while the provider says so. Print static. Rent dynamic knowingly.

free_alternatives.name: QRCode Monkey
free_alternatives.why: Free static QR codes with logos and colors — no expiry, no account

listings.name: QRCode Monkey
listings.limits: Static codes only on the free tool
listings.realcost: Nothing. Ad-supported site; the codes themselves are yours forever.
listings.short: Yes — free static QR codes with logos, colors, and high-resolution download, no account, no expiry, no scan limits. The code is baked with your link and works as long as your link does.
listings.catch: The site earns from ads and an upsell to a paid dynamic-code product — clearly separated from the free tool. The static codes it makes are permanent by physics, not by promise: nothing to expire, no service to lapse.

Custom colors, logos, and design
High-resolution downloads (PNG, SVG, PDF, EPS)
No signup, no expiration, no scan limits
paid_locked: Dynamic (editable, trackable) codes — their separate paid product
drawbacks: Static means uneditable — a typo'd link means a new code
No scan analytics on static codes
The site's ads and upsells sit around the free tool
listings.get_summary: In practice: the safe default for anything printed — business cards, menus, signage — because nothing behind the code can ever be switched off.
listings.worth: Dynamic-QR subscriptions run $5–15/month; for permanent links, this does the job at $0 forever.
smart_moves: Triple-check the URL before generating — static codes can't be edited after
Download the SVG or EPS for print; PNG for screens
Test-scan the final printed proof, not just the file
more_info: Why static codes can't be taken from you

A static QR code encodes your URL directly in the pattern — no middleman server, no account, no subscription keeping it alive. Once printed, only two things can break it: physical damage or your own destination page going down. That immunity is the whole reason this listing exists next to the trap above it.
When you genuinely need dynamic

If the destination must change after printing, or you need scan counts, dynamic is the right tool — as a knowing paid purchase from a stable provider, never as a free-trial artifact on printed material.

listings.name: "Free" Logo Makers (Looka, Tailor Brands & co.)
listings.limits: Design free; downloading the logo costs money
listings.realcost: Your design time. The logo you 'made free' is ransomed at the download button.
listings.short: No — and this is the internet's most polished fake. 'Free logo maker' means the designing is free; the moment you want your finished logo, Looka charges $20–65 one-time and Tailor Brands puts a usable download behind a subscription. The hour you invested is the pressure to pay.
listings.catch: The pattern is sunk-cost by design: generate, customize, fall in love, then meet the price at checkout. Tailor Brands draws extra fire in reviews for one-month signups landing as annual subscriptions. Neither hides the price forever — they just show it after your effort, not before.
free_includes: Unlimited designing, previewing, and tweaking
Tailor Brands: a low-resolution, watermarked sample download
paid_locked: The actual usable files — high-resolution, vector, transparent
Commercial rights arrive with the purchase, not the design
drawbacks: The word 'free' describes the labor, not the logo
Subscription-model makers (Tailor Brands) beat one-time makers at generating complaints
Hours of customization become the sales pressure
listings.get_summary: In practice: a free design toy attached to a paid file store. Know the price before you invest the hour, and it's a fair cheap-logo service; discover it after, and it's a hostage exchange.
listings.worth: A finished logo here costs $20–96/year depending on maker; genuinely free routes exist one tab over.
smart_moves: Genuinely free logos exist: Canva's free logo templates, Shopify's free logo maker, and Squarespace's logo maker all allow free downloads
If you use Looka, know it's a one-time $20+ purchase — the fairest of the breed
With Tailor Brands, read whether that 'month' is billed as a year before entering a card
Check the license line: 'free' downloads elsewhere are sometimes non-commercial only
more_info: The sunk-cost machine, explained

These tools are engineered so payment arrives at the moment of maximum attachment: after naming, styling, and previewing your logo on a storefront. Classic behavioral design. The counter-move is deciding your budget before opening the tool — $0 means starting at Canva, not here.
What you're actually buying

The purchase typically includes high-resolution and vector files plus commercial rights — legitimate deliverables at a fair price for a template-built mark. The verdict here punishes the 'free' framing, not the product: sold honestly as a $20 logo shop, this listing would read very differently.

free_alternatives.name: Canva
free_alternatives.why: Free logo templates with actual free downloads — no ransom at checkout

listings.name: Unsplash
listings.realcost: Nothing for the free library; premium images now salt the search results.
listings.short: Yes — millions of high-resolution photos, free to download and use commercially without attribution under the Unsplash license, no account required. It remains one of the great honest free resources of the internet.
listings.catch: Since Getty's acquisition, paid 'Unsplash+' images increasingly appear atop search results with the free ones below — the familiar crown-content pattern. The free library underneath is still vast and still genuinely free.
free_includes: Millions of high-resolution photos
Commercial use allowed, no attribution required (license terms apply)
Download without an account
A license far simpler than traditional stock sites
paid_locked: Unsplash+ premium images mixed into search results
Legal indemnification and model-released certainty at stock-agency level
drawbacks: Popular free images appear on thousands of other sites
No guarantee of model/property releases — care needed for ads featuring people
Premium results increasingly crowd the top of searches
listings.get_summary: In practice: the default first stop for website and content imagery — professional photos at zero cost, minus the uniqueness and legal armor of paid stock.
listings.worth: Traditional stock sites charge $10+ per image or $30+/month; this library is free by license, not by trial.
smart_moves: Filter or scroll past the Unsplash+ rows — the free library sits right below
Reverse-image-search a photo before building a brand on it; you'll see how many sites share it
For ads featuring recognizable people, use released stock — the free license doesn't certify releases
Pexels and Pixabay are the sibling free libraries when searches run dry
more_info: The license, in plain words

The Unsplash license grants free commercial and noncommercial use without attribution, with a few carve-outs (like selling the photos as-is). It's radically simpler than rights-managed stock — the trade is that it certifies less, particularly around model releases for commercial endorsement contexts.
The Getty era

Getty's acquisition brought Unsplash+ — paid, exclusive, fully released imagery threaded into results. The free library remains enormous; just recognize the search page is now also a storefront, and scroll accordingly.

listings.name: Adobe Stock Free Trial
listings.limits: 10 standard assets in 30 days, then billed monthly
listings.realcost: Auto-converts to a paid monthly subscription; the price of forgetting is steep.
listings.short: No. The '10 free images' require a card and enroll you in a subscription that bills automatically at day 30 — around thirty dollars a month for the standard plan. The images are the bait; the billing date is the business.
listings.catch: One genuine sweetener mirrors Audible: assets licensed during the trial remain licensed after you cancel. Played precisely — download ten, calendar the exit — it's a real haul. Played asleep, it's the most expensive stock subscription you never meant to have.
free_includes: 10 standard stock assets during the 30-day trial — card required
Licenses for trial downloads survive cancellation
paid_locked: Everything after day 30, at a recurring monthly price
Premium and video assets, which the trial excludes
drawbacks: Card and auto-bill mechanics from minute one
Unused trial downloads don't bank or roll over
Annual-plan variants can carry Adobe's cancellation-fee structure
listings.get_summary: In practice: ten professional images free — for people who set alarms. Everyone else donates a month or more of subscription fees to forgetting.
listings.worth: Ten standard stock images bought individually run $80–100+; the trial delivers that value if the exit executes.
smart_moves: Download all 10 assets in the first sitting — value banked, decision deferred
Calendar day 27; cancel on the website and screenshot confirmation
Choose the monthly plan at signup, never 'annual billed monthly,' so no termination fee can exist
Unsplash covers most needs first — spend the trial only on images free libraries can't match
more_info: The keep-your-downloads rule

Standard assets licensed during the trial stay licensed after cancellation — Adobe's genuine sweetener. The strategy writes itself: harvest immediately, exit on schedule, keep the receipts. The trap only functions on people who graze slowly.
Watch the plan type at signup

Like Creative Cloud, Stock offers 'annual, billed monthly' — cheaper-looking, but mid-year cancellation triggers Adobe's early-termination fee structure. The pure monthly plan costs a little more and can be exited any month clean. On a trial you intend to cancel, that difference is everything.

free_alternatives.name: Unsplash
free_alternatives.why: Millions of photos, free for commercial use, no account needed

listings.name: Photopea
listings.realcost: Nothing. Ad-supported, runs in the browser.
listings.short: Yes — Photopea is a full Photoshop-class image editor that runs in your browser with no download, no account, and no install. It opens actual PSD files.
listings.catch: Ads sit beside the canvas. A paid plan removes them, but the free tool is identical in features — nothing is withheld.
free_includes: Full layer-based image editing in the browser
Opens PSD, XCF, Sketch, AI, and XD files natively
Every export format including PSD back out
No install, no account, no download
drawbacks: Sidebar ads while you work
Requires internet — no offline mode
One developer's project — bus-factor risk
listings.get_summary: In practice: the tool that makes Adobe's trial trap unnecessary. Real PSD editing, free, right now, in a tab.
listings.worth: ≈ $270/year — what Photoshop costs, at $0 in a browser tab.
smart_moves: Bookmark photopea.com — no app store needed
It opens real .psd files; try it before paying for Photoshop
Use it as the escape hatch when Adobe's trial ends
more_info: How can one person give away Photoshop?

Photopea was built and is maintained by a solo developer, funded by the sidebar ads. The economics work because there's no team, no office, and no sales force — just a browser tab that does what a $270/year subscription does. The ads are the entire business model, and removing them is the only paid option.
PSD compatibility is the killer feature

Most free editors can't open Photoshop files. Photopea reads and writes them natively, which means it slots into professional workflows as a free link in the chain — not a replacement ecosystem, just a tool that works.

listings.name: DaVinci Resolve
listings.realcost: Nothing. The free version is used on real Hollywood productions.
listings.short: Yes — DaVinci Resolve is a professional video editor, color grading suite, visual effects tool, and audio post-production app in one — genuinely free, not a trial.
listings.catch: A paid Studio version exists with extra features (GPU acceleration, HDR tools, collaboration), but the free version covers the full editing and color workflow that made the software famous.
free_includes: Professional nonlinear video editing
The industry-standard color grading tools
Fairlight audio post-production
Fusion visual effects and motion graphics
Unlimited export resolution
paid_locked: Some advanced noise reduction and effects
drawbacks: Requires a capable computer — it's professional software
The learning curve matches the power
Large install size
listings.get_summary: In practice: the only free video editor that professionals actually use on paid work. Everything from YouTube videos to feature films.
listings.worth: The paid Studio version costs $295 one-time; the free version covers most workflows entirely.
smart_moves: Start with the 'Cut' page — it's the simplified editing mode built for speed
YouTube tutorials from Blackmagic themselves are free and excellent
The free version exports at full resolution with no watermark — verify this is real by exporting once
more_info: Why would Blackmagic give this away?

Blackmagic makes hardware — cameras, capture cards, control panels. Resolve is free because it sells the ecosystem. A free editor that professionals love creates demand for the hardware it runs best on. The incentive alignment is the same as Google Maps: the free product IS the business strategy.
Free vs. Studio — the honest split

Free covers editing, color, audio, and effects. Studio adds speed (multi-GPU), depth (HDR, advanced noise reduction), and scale (multi-user projects). Most solo creators and small teams never need Studio; large post-houses do.
listings.canada: Available worldwide. Blackmagic has offices in Melbourne — no Canadian restrictions.

listings.name: VS Code
listings.realcost: Nothing. Microsoft's open-source code editor.
listings.short: Yes — Visual Studio Code is Microsoft's code editor: free, open source, with an extension ecosystem that covers every programming language. Used by the majority of professional developers.
listings.catch: There is no catch. VS Code is Microsoft's play for developer mindshare and Azure adoption — the editor is the front door, not the product.
free_includes: A full code editor with IntelliSense, debugging, and Git integration
Thousands of free extensions for every language and framework
Runs on Windows, Mac, and Linux
Built-in terminal, source control, and remote development
drawbacks: Telemetry is on by default — disable it in settings
The extension marketplace can be overwhelming
Microsoft's data collection policies apply unless you use the open-source VSCodium build
listings.get_summary: In practice: the default tool professional developers reach for — powerful enough for any project, free enough for any student.
listings.worth: Professional IDEs run $100–300/year; this covers most developers' needs at $0.
smart_moves: Turn off telemetry in settings if data collection bothers you
Install the language extensions you need — Python, JavaScript, etc. — they're all free
For full privacy, use VSCodium — the same editor without Microsoft telemetry
more_info: Why Microsoft gives away a code editor

Developers who use VS Code write code that runs on Azure. The editor is the top of a funnel that ends at cloud revenue — generous because the generosity pays elsewhere. Same pattern as Google Maps, Blender's foundation, and every free tool on this site that's backed by a larger business.

listings.name: Signal
listings.realcost: Nothing. Nonprofit, donation-funded.
listings.short: Yes — Signal is end-to-end encrypted messaging, calls, and video — free, no ads, no tracking, funded by donations. The protocol itself is used by WhatsApp and others.
listings.catch: There is no catch. Signal is a nonprofit that exists to provide private communication. Your phone number is the only identifier.
free_includes: End-to-end encrypted text, voice, and video
Group chats and disappearing messages
No ads, no tracking, no data harvesting
The encryption protocol other apps license
drawbacks: Requires a phone number to register
Smaller user base than WhatsApp or iMessage
No business/marketing features
listings.get_summary: In practice: the most private mainstream messenger — the one journalists, activists, and security professionals actually use.
listings.worth: Privacy-focused services charge $5–10/month; this delivers stronger privacy at $0.
smart_moves: Make it your default and invite contacts — the network effect is the only barrier
Turn on disappearing messages for sensitive conversations
Your phone number is your identity — use a secondary number if that matters
more_info: Who pays for Signal?

The Signal Foundation, a nonprofit funded by donations and a large initial endowment from Signal co-founder and WhatsApp co-founder Brian Acton. No ads, no investors, no data monetization — the structure is designed so the incentive to spy on you literally doesn't exist.
listings.canada: Available worldwide. No geographic restrictions.

listings.name: WhatsApp
listings.realcost: Nothing in money. Meta owns your metadata.
listings.short: Yes — WhatsApp is free for personal messaging, calls, and video worldwide, with end-to-end encryption on message content. No subscription, no ads inside chats.
listings.catch: Meta (Facebook) owns WhatsApp and monetizes the business platform side. Personal use is free; the cost is that metadata — who you talk to, when, how often — flows into Meta's ecosystem even though message content is encrypted.
free_includes: End-to-end encrypted messaging and calls
Group chats up to 1,024 people
Voice and video calls worldwide
File and location sharing
drawbacks: Metadata (who, when, how often) goes to Meta
Phone number required
Tied to the Meta/Facebook ecosystem
listings.get_summary: In practice: the world's default messenger outside the US — three billion users, free calls across borders, no subscription.
listings.worth: International calling alone saves heavy users $20–50/month vs. traditional plans.
smart_moves: Review the privacy settings — limit who sees your status, photo, and last-seen
Know the distinction: message content is encrypted; metadata is not
For maximum privacy, use Signal instead — same encryption, no Meta
more_info: End-to-end encryption vs. metadata

WhatsApp encrypts what you say; Meta collects data about who you say it to, when, how often, and from where. For most people this trade is fine. For people whose contact patterns themselves are sensitive, it isn't — and Signal is the free alternative that collects neither.
listings.canada: Available in Canada. Widely used for international calls to family abroad — free over WiFi.

listings.name: Telegram
listings.realcost: Nothing in money. Ad-supported channels; Premium exists.
listings.short: Yes — Telegram is free for messaging, groups, channels, and file sharing with generous limits. A paid Premium tier exists but is optional.
listings.catch: Regular chats are not end-to-end encrypted by default — only 'Secret Chats' are. The free tier is genuine and generous; the privacy model is weaker than Signal or WhatsApp for regular messages.
free_includes: Unlimited messaging and groups up to 200,000 members
Channels for broadcasting to unlimited subscribers
Up to 2GB file sharing per file
Cloud sync across all devices
paid_locked: Premium: larger uploads, faster downloads, exclusive reactions
drawbacks: Regular chats are NOT end-to-end encrypted by default
Phone number required to register
Data stored on Telegram's servers, accessible to Telegram
listings.get_summary: In practice: the most feature-rich free messenger — giant groups, huge file transfers, channels — with a privacy trade-off most users don't realize.
listings.worth: Group and channel features that rival paid platforms like Slack or Discord, at $0.
smart_moves: Use Secret Chats for anything sensitive — those are end-to-end encrypted
Regular chats are encrypted in transit but Telegram holds the keys
For true privacy, use Signal; for features and reach, Telegram wins
more_info: The encryption distinction that matters

WhatsApp and Signal encrypt everything end-to-end by default. Telegram encrypts regular chats in transit but stores them on its servers with keys Telegram holds. Only 'Secret Chats' — a separate mode you must manually start — get end-to-end encryption. For most social chatting this is fine; for anything sensitive, it's the wrong default.

listings.name: Bitwarden
listings.realcost: An account. The free tier covers what most people need from a password manager.
listings.short: Yes — Bitwarden's free plan stores unlimited passwords across unlimited devices with full encryption, no card, no time limit. It's the rare security product where free doesn't mean compromised.
listings.catch: Premium adds conveniences (hardware key support, vault health reports, emergency access) but the core — unlimited encrypted passwords synced everywhere — is permanently free.
free_includes: Unlimited passwords in an encrypted vault
Sync across all devices — phone, desktop, browser
Secure password generator
End-to-end encryption; open source and audited
drawbacks: Premium features are genuinely useful for power users
The interface is functional, not beautiful
Self-hosting is possible but requires technical comfort
listings.get_summary: In practice: a real password manager that security professionals recommend — unlimited, encrypted, cross-device — at the price most people need: zero.
listings.worth: Competing password managers charge $36–60/year; this covers the core need free.
smart_moves: Install the browser extension and phone app — sync is the whole point
Import passwords from your browser or old manager on day one
Enable two-factor authentication on the Bitwarden account itself
more_info: Why trust a free password manager?

Bitwarden is open source — the code is public, independently audited, and verifiable. The business model is straightforward: free for individuals, paid for teams and enterprises. Unlike closed-source alternatives, you don't have to take their word for the encryption — anyone can inspect it.
Bitwarden vs. the browser's built-in passwords

Chrome and Safari save passwords for free too, but they lock you into one ecosystem and offer weaker sharing, auditing, and cross-platform support. Bitwarden is the step up that costs nothing and works everywhere.
listings.canada: Available worldwide including Canada. Free tier identical.

listings.name: WordPress.com
listings.limits: WordPress ads on your site; no custom domain
listings.realcost: An account. Your site advertises WordPress until you pay.
listings.short: Sort of. WordPress.com's free plan hosts a real blog or site — but with WordPress ads on your pages, a .wordpress.com address, limited storage, and no plugins or custom themes.
listings.catch: The free tier is a live demo of the WordPress experience, designed to make the limitations visible enough that you upgrade. The self-hosted WordPress.org software (different product) is genuinely free with no such limits.
free_includes: A hosted blog or website with basic themes
A .wordpress.com subdomain
Limited storage
Basic customization
drawbacks: WordPress.com ads appear on your free site
The .wordpress.com address looks amateur
Very limited theme and plugin access
Confusingly similar name to WordPress.org (which is truly free software)
listings.get_summary: In practice: enough to start a personal blog and learn WordPress — not enough to run a business or look professional.
listings.worth: Paid WordPress.com plans start at $4–8/month; self-hosted WordPress.org on cheap hosting costs about the same and gives full control.
smart_moves: For a real free website, Netlify hosting with a static site or self-hosted WordPress.org on free-tier hosting gives you more control
Don't confuse WordPress.com (hosted, limited free) with WordPress.org (the free open-source software you host yourself)
If you stay free here, treat it as a writing sandbox, not a public brand
more_info: WordPress.com vs. WordPress.org — the confusion that costs money

WordPress.org is free, open-source software you download and host yourself — full control, any theme, any plugin. WordPress.com is a hosted service run by Automattic that uses that software but adds its own tiers and restrictions. The .com version trades control for convenience; the .org version is genuinely free but requires you to find hosting. Most 'is WordPress free' searches land on .com and never learn about .org.

listings.name: TurboTax Free Edition
listings.limits: Only ~37% of filers qualify; simple Form 1040 returns only
listings.realcost: Nothing if you qualify — but 63% of filers discover they don't, often after entering all their data.
listings.short: Sort of — and this is a famous case. TurboTax Free Edition is real and files both federal and state returns at $0, but only about 37% of filers qualify: simple W-2 income, standard deduction, no investments, no freelance work. The FTC ruled Intuit's 'free' advertising deceptive because most people who started the 'free' flow were forced into paid tiers.
listings.catch: The bait-and-switch is the product design: you enter your personal data, proceed through the flow, and discover mid-return that your situation requires Deluxe ($39+), Premium ($69+), or Self-Employed ($119+). By then your time investment creates the pressure to pay. The FTC's finding of deceptive advertising wasn't about the fine print — it was about the experience.
free_includes: Federal and state filing for simple Form 1040 returns
W-2 income, standard deduction, and select credits (EITC, Child Tax Credit)
Step-by-step guided interface
E-filing included
paid_locked: Any return with investments, freelance income, itemized deductions, or rental income
drawbacks: 63% of filers get forced into paid tiers mid-return
The FTC found Intuit's 'free' advertising deceptive
No support included — even basic help is a paid upgrade
Intuit left the IRS Free File program in 2021
listings.get_summary: In practice: a genuinely free tax filing for people with the simplest possible returns — a single W-2, standard deduction, no investments. Everyone else is a conversion target.
listings.worth: Paid TurboTax tiers run $39–$119+ for federal alone; the free tier saves that if you qualify.
smart_moves: Before starting: do you have only W-2 income and take the standard deduction? If yes, you qualify. If no, don't waste time entering data
Cash App Taxes (formerly Credit Karma Tax) covers more situations free — check it first
IRS Free File partners offer free guided filing if your AGI is under the threshold — start at irs.gov/freefile
FreeTaxUSA charges $0 federal for all complexity levels and ~$15 for state — often the best deal for non-simple returns
more_info: The FTC ruling, explained

In January 2024 the FTC ruled that Intuit engaged in deceptive advertising by running ubiquitous 'free' ads for a product most people couldn't use free. The order prohibited future deceptive 'free' claims. The free edition still exists and works — for the 37% — but the advertising that drove millions of ineligible filers into the flow was the violation.
Why Intuit left IRS Free File

Intuit participated in the IRS Free File program from 2003 until 2021, then withdrew — citing 'limitations.' Critics noted that the program required genuine free filing for low-income taxpayers, which competed with Intuit's paid tiers. The withdrawal meant TurboTax's 'free' became Intuit's definition, not the IRS's.
The truly free alternatives

IRS Free File (irs.gov/freefile) offers guided free filing through partner software if your AGI is under the threshold. Cash App Taxes files most returns completely free. FreeTaxUSA charges $0 federal regardless of complexity. All three cover more situations than TurboTax Free Edition — the main thing TurboTax sells is the brand and the interface.

free_alternatives.name: Credit Karma
free_alternatives.why: Free credit monitoring and tax filing through Cash App Taxes

free_alternatives.name: AnnualCreditReport.com
free_alternatives.why: Your official credit reports, free by federal law

listings.canada: TurboTax offers a free tier in Canada (TurboTax Free) for simple returns. Wealthsimple Tax (formerly SimpleTax) is the Canadian equivalent of FreeTaxUSA — genuinely free for all return types, pay-what-you-want model.

listings.name: Cash App
listings.limits: Fees on instant transfers and credit card funding
listings.realcost: An account and your transaction data. Instant transfers cost a fee.
listings.short: Yes — sending and receiving money between Cash App users is genuinely free with no subscription. The fees live in specific optional features: instant transfers to your bank, funding from a credit card, and Bitcoin transactions.
listings.catch: Cash App is free the way a bank account is free — the basic service costs nothing, and specific transactions carry fees. The company also earns from Cash App Taxes (free filing), the Cash Card (interchange revenue), and Bitcoin spreads.
free_includes: Free person-to-person payments
Free standard bank transfers (1-3 business days)
A free Visa debit card (Cash Card) with occasional 'Boost' discounts
Cash App Taxes: free tax filing for most return types
drawbacks: Instant transfers cost money — standard is free but slow
A frequent target for scams; no purchase protection like PayPal
Customer support is notoriously hard to reach
listings.get_summary: In practice: free peer-to-peer payments and a surprisingly capable free tax filing service, wrapped in an app that makes money on speed and crypto.
listings.worth: Competing payment services are similarly free for basics; the bundled free tax filing is the unusual value-add.
smart_moves: Always use 'Standard' transfer to avoid fees — it takes 1-3 days but costs nothing
Never send money to strangers — Cash App has no buyer protection
Cash App Taxes (formerly Credit Karma Tax) is a genuinely free tax filing tool baked into the app
more_info: The scam vulnerability

Cash App payments are instant and irreversible, with no buyer protection. The free service attracts scammers because there's no chargeback mechanism. Send money only to people you know and trust — this is a feature of the product's design, not a bug.
listings.canada: Not available in Canada. Canadian equivalents for peer-to-peer payments: Interac e-Transfer (free through most banks) and Wealthsimple Cash.

listings.name: Venmo
listings.limits: Fees on instant transfers and credit card funding
listings.realcost: An account. Standard transfers are free; speed and credit cards cost extra.
listings.short: Yes — Venmo is free for sending and receiving money between users via bank account or debit card. Instant transfers and credit card funding carry fees, same as Cash App.
listings.catch: PayPal (Venmo's parent) monetizes the business payment side, instant transfers, and the Venmo credit card. Personal use between friends is genuinely free.
free_includes: Free person-to-person payments via bank or debit
A social feed of transactions (hideable)
Free standard bank transfers (1-3 days)
A Venmo debit card
drawbacks: The social feed defaults to public — change this immediately
Instant transfers cost money
Not designed for buyer protection — friends only
listings.get_summary: In practice: splitting dinner and paying rent, free. The social feed is the quirk; the payments are the product.
listings.worth: The same free peer-to-peer payments as Cash App, with a social layer.
smart_moves: Set your default privacy to 'Private' on day one — the social feed defaults to public
Use standard transfer to avoid fees
Don't use Venmo for purchases from strangers — no buyer protection
more_info: The privacy default nobody changes
Venmo's transaction feed defaults to public — meaning anyone can see who you paid and the memo. Most users never change this. Go to Settings → Privacy → Private before your first transaction.
listings.canada: Not available in Canada. Use Interac e-Transfer — free, instant, built into Canadian banking.

listings.name: PayPal
listings.limits: Fees on receiving business payments and currency conversion
listings.realcost: An account. Personal domestic transfers are free; everything commercial has fees.
listings.short: Yes for personal use — sending money to friends and family from your bank account or PayPal balance is free domestically. Business payments, international transfers, and currency conversion all carry fees.
listings.catch: PayPal makes money on the business side (merchant fees), international transfers, and currency conversion. The personal peer-to-peer product is the free front door to a payments empire.
free_includes: Free domestic person-to-person transfers from bank/balance
Buyer protection on purchases
A PayPal debit card
Bill splitting and request features
drawbacks: Credit card-funded personal payments carry fees
Currency conversion spreads are significant
Account freezes and holds have a notorious reputation
Customer service complaints are legendary
listings.get_summary: In practice: free domestic money transfers plus genuine buyer protection on purchases — the protection alone justifies having an account.
listings.worth: Buyer protection on purchases is the unique free value — no other peer-to-peer app matches it.
smart_moves: Fund from bank account, not credit card, to keep personal payments free
Use PayPal for purchases where buyer protection matters — that's its edge over Cash App and Venmo
Watch the exchange rate on international payments — the spread is where they earn
more_info: Buyer protection is the real product

Unlike Cash App and Venmo, PayPal offers dispute resolution and refund protection on purchases. For buying from strangers — eBay, online stores, freelancers — this is the free feature that actually matters and the reason to maintain a PayPal account even if you prefer other apps for splitting bills.
listings.canada: Available in Canada. Same free personal transfers; FX fees on cross-border payments are higher than in the US.

listings.name: Netflix
listings.realcost: There is no free version. Every plan is paid.
listings.short: No. Despite being the most-searched streaming service, Netflix has no free tier, no ad-supported free plan, and no trial. Every plan requires a card and bills monthly.
listings.catch: Netflix briefly tested free plans in some markets years ago and discontinued them. The ad-supported tier is cheaper but still paid. There is no legal way to watch Netflix without a subscription.
free_includes: Nothing — every plan is paid
paid_locked: The entire catalog, starting at the ad-supported tier price
drawbacks: No free tier exists
No trial period
Every plan requires a credit card and auto-renews
listings.get_summary: In practice: there is no free Netflix to explain. The question is what you're getting from genuinely free streaming instead.
listings.worth: $0 — there is nothing free here. Plans start around $7–8/month with ads.
smart_moves: Don't search for 'free Netflix' — those sites are scams or credential theft
Tubi and Pluto TV offer real free streaming with no card
Kanopy and Hoopla through your library are ad-free and genuinely free

more_info: Why no free tier?

Netflix's content costs billions annually in licensing and production. Unlike ad-supported free services (Tubi, Pluto TV), Netflix's model requires subscriber revenue to fund originals. The economics don't support free — and the brand is strong enough that they don't need a free door.

listings.catch: The one-device-type restriction, introduced in 2021, was the cut that drove millions of users to Bitwarden. Combined with a major 2022 security breach, LastPass's free tier is now the cautionary tale of this category.

more_info: The 2022 breach, in context

Attackers accessed encrypted password vaults. LastPass says the encryption holds if your master password was strong; security researchers have noted that older accounts may have had weaker encryption defaults. The breach didn't end LastPass, but it shifted the trust calculation permanently — especially when a free, audited, open-source alternative exists.

The Wikimedia Foundation runs on roughly $150–170 million annually in donations, with no ads and no paywall. The donation banners are annoying on purpose; they're the entire revenue model.
