deals.who: Students enrolled at eligible institutions (verified via UNiDAYS)
deals.how: Subscribe through Apple Music and verify student status via UNiDAYS
deals.worth: ~$6/month savings plus free Apple TV+ (normally $10/month) — roughly $190/year in total value
deals.caveat: Re-verification required periodically. Maxes out at 48 months total.

deals.name: Spotify Student
deals.summary: Verified students get Spotify Premium at a discount, often bundled with Hulu (ad-supported) and Showtime in the US.
deals.who: Students enrolled at accredited US institutions
deals.how: Subscribe at spotify.com/student and verify via SheerID
deals.worth: Premium plus streaming services at a fraction of buying each separately
deals.caveat: Re-verification every 12 months; limit of 4 years total. Bundle components and pricing change by region.

deals.name: JetBrains Student
deals.summary: Verified students and teachers get every JetBrains IDE — IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, the full suite — free for the duration of their studies.
deals.who: Students and teachers at accredited institutions
deals.how: Apply at jetbrains.com/community/education with school email, ISIC card, or official document
deals.worth: The All Products Pack runs $289/year at full price — every IDE, free
deals.caveat: For non-commercial, educational use only. Annual renewal with re-verification required.

deals.name: LinkedIn Learning via Library
deals.summary: Many public libraries include full LinkedIn Learning access — the same ~$30/month product — free with a library card, no trial, no credit card, no expiry.
deals.who: Anyone with a card at a participating public library
deals.how: Visit your library's website, look under digital resources or databases for LinkedIn Learning
deals.worth: ~$360/year — the full course library with certificates that post to your LinkedIn profile
deals.caveat: Not every library participates; check yours first. Access is via the library portal, not linkedin.com directly.

deals.name: Adobe Creative Cloud Student
deals.summary: Verified students and teachers get the full Creative Cloud suite at roughly 60% off the standard price for the first year.
deals.who: Students 13+ and teachers at accredited institutions
deals.how: Purchase through adobe.com/creativecloud/plans with student verification
deals.worth: Saves roughly $420 in the first year versus the standard All Apps price
deals.caveat: First-year pricing jumps to a higher (still discounted) rate at renewal — read the renewal price before committing. The annual plan's early-termination fee still applies.

deals.name: Audible 3-month trial (seasonal)
deals.summary: Audible periodically offers 3-month free trials (vs. the standard 30-day) during Prime Day, holidays, and promotional events. Three credits instead of one — and all books claimed stay yours.
deals.who: New Audible subscribers (sometimes returning subscribers after a gap)
deals.how: Watch for the offer during Prime Day (July), Black Friday, and holiday sales; the landing page changes
deals.worth: 3 premium audiobooks ($45–75 retail value) kept forever, even after cancelling
deals.caveat: Seasonal and unpredictable; the standard offer is 1 month / 1 credit. Same auto-billing mechanics apply — alarm the end date regardless of trial length.

deals.name: NordVPN / Surfshark / ExpressVPN sales
deals.summary: The major VPN providers perpetually advertise 70–85% off, but the discount applies only to 2-year prepaid plans — the monthly rate after that period reverts to full price unless you cancel.
deals.who: Anyone — these are the standard offers, not special promotions
deals.how: Visit any VPN provider's homepage; the 'sale' is always running
deals.worth: Real savings vs. monthly billing — but only for the prepaid term. The headline rate is the commitment price, not the renewal price.
deals.caveat: The '83% off' is real math but misleading framing: you're comparing a 2-year prepaid rate to a monthly rate nobody pays. Read the renewal price before committing, and calendar the renewal date — VPN auto-renewals at full price are one of the most common surprise charges online.

deals.name: Costco membership cashback
deals.summary: Costco's membership comes with an unconditional satisfaction guarantee: cancel at any time for a full refund of your membership fee, no questions asked.
deals.who: Anyone considering or holding a Costco membership
deals.how: Cancel at any membership desk or through your online account
deals.worth: The entire membership fee back — the 'risk-free trial' that's actually risk-free because no card trap exists. The Executive membership additionally earns 2% back on purchases.
deals.caveat: Refunding after using the membership all year is technically allowed but tests the spirit of the policy. The Executive tier's 2% reward is capped annually.

deals.name: SCORE Business Mentoring
deals.type: Free ongoing business mentoring — truly free
deals.summary: 10,000 volunteer business mentors, backed by the U.S. Small Business Administration, offering free one-on-one advising by phone, video, email, or in person. Not a single session — for the life of your business. No catch, no upsell, no fee.
deals.who: Anyone in the US with a business idea, a startup, or an existing business at any stage
deals.how: Enter your zip code at score.org/find-mentor, fill out a request form, and get matched with a mentor in your industry
deals.worth: Business consulting runs $150–400/hour. SCORE mentors provide ongoing guidance — the equivalent of thousands in advisory fees, free, indefinitely.
deals.caveat: Mentor quality varies — they're volunteers with real experience, but expertise ranges. You can request a different mentor at any time. US-based businesses only.

deals.name: SBA Small Business Development Centers (SBDCs)
deals.summary: Nearly 1,000 SBDC locations nationwide offering free, confidential one-on-one business consulting on planning, financing, marketing, management, and operations. Funded by the SBA and hosted at universities and colleges.
deals.who: Any current or aspiring small business owner in the US
deals.how: Find your local SBDC at sba.gov and schedule an appointment — virtual or in-person
deals.worth: Professional business consulting at $0, covering areas where private consultants charge $100–300/hour. Advisors have real industry and academic credentials.
deals.caveat: Availability varies by location and demand. Sessions focus on advising, not doing the work for you. Some locations have waiting lists during peak times.

deals.name: ABA Free Legal Answers
deals.type: Free legal advice from volunteer attorneys
deals.summary: The American Bar Association's pro bono portal where income-eligible individuals submit civil legal questions online and receive written answers from volunteer attorneys licensed in their state. Nearly 500,000 questions answered to date.
deals.who: Income-eligible individuals in the US with civil (non-criminal) legal questions
deals.how: Create an account at abafreelegalanswers.org, verify income eligibility, and submit your legal question
deals.worth: A single hour of legal advice runs $200–500+. This provides targeted written guidance from a licensed attorney at $0.
deals.caveat: Civil questions only — no criminal, immigration (separate federal portal exists), or ongoing representation. Answers are brief legal guidance, not full legal representation. Income eligibility requirements apply.

deals.name: VITA Free Tax Preparation
deals.type: Free in-person tax filing by IRS-certified volunteers
deals.summary: The IRS Volunteer Income Tax Assistance program provides free tax preparation by certified volunteers at community locations nationwide — for people who earn roughly $67,000 or less, have disabilities, or have limited English. A real human prepares and files your return at $0.
deals.who: Individuals and families earning approximately $67,000 or less, people with disabilities, and limited-English speakers
deals.how: Find a VITA site near you using the IRS locator tool at irs.gov or by calling 800-906-9887
deals.worth: Tax preparation fees run $150–500+ depending on complexity. VITA covers the same service at $0, done by a trained, IRS-certified human.
deals.caveat: Seasonal — operates January through mid-April at most sites. Some locations serve year-round. Bring all tax documents. Wait times can be long during peak season.

deals.name: Apple Store Free Sessions (Today at Apple)
deals.type: Free creative workshops and one-on-one help
deals.summary: Every Apple Store offers free daily sessions: creative workshops on photography, music, video, design, and coding, plus free one-on-one personal setup and troubleshooting for any Apple product — no purchase required for the workshops.
deals.who: Anyone — no Apple purchase required for workshop attendance
deals.how: Browse and register at apple.com/today or walk into any Apple Store
deals.worth: Creative workshops and tech support at $0 in a beautifully designed space. Private tutoring in these subjects runs $50–100/hour.
deals.caveat: Workshops teach Apple's ecosystem (iPhone photography, GarageBand, etc.) — genuine education, but inside Apple's world. One-on-one Genius Bar support for repairs may lead to paid service recommendations.

deals.name: HubSpot Free Business Courses & Certifications
deals.type: Free marketing, sales, and business certifications
deals.summary: HubSpot Academy offers free courses and industry-recognized certifications in digital marketing, content strategy, sales, SEO, and CRM — taught by real practitioners, with no card and no trial clock.
deals.who: Anyone — no HubSpot product purchase required
deals.how: Create a free HubSpot Academy account and start any course or certification track
deals.worth: Comparable marketing certifications from other providers run $200–500. HubSpot's are free and genuinely respected in the industry.
deals.caveat: The courses naturally feature HubSpot's own tools — genuine education inside their ecosystem. Certifications require periodic renewal. The knowledge transfers; the tool-specific parts are the upsell.

deals.name: Shopify Free Business Courses (Shopify Learn)
deals.summary: Shopify Learn offers free courses on starting an online business, product photography, social media marketing, and e-commerce fundamentals — no Shopify subscription required to take them.
deals.who: Anyone — no Shopify account needed for most courses
deals.how: Browse courses at shopify.com/learn and start watching
deals.worth: E-commerce courses elsewhere run $50–200. These are free and taught by successful Shopify merchants.
deals.caveat: The courses naturally point toward building on Shopify — real education, inside their funnel. The business knowledge transfers regardless of platform.

deals.name: Google for Small Business (free tools & coaching)
deals.type: Free digital skills training and business tools
deals.summary: Google bundles its free small business tools — Business Profile, Analytics, Search Console, and digital skills training — into one portal with personalized recommendations, coaching resources, and free workshops.
deals.who: Any small business owner
deals.how: Visit smallbusiness.withgoogle.com and follow the guided setup for your business type
deals.worth: A digital marketing agency charges thousands to set up what Google gives away for free here — because your business activity on these tools feeds Google's ad ecosystem.
deals.caveat: Every free tool ultimately serves Google's ad business. The tools are genuinely useful; the ecosystem they build is the product. Use them knowingly.

deals.name: DreamSite Design — Free 30-Minute Consultation
deals.summary: A free 30-minute consultation with a DreamSite Design web strategist — site audits, redesign planning, new builds, or just honest answers about what your site needs. No obligation, no pitch timer. Use code VERIFREEDREAM when booking.
deals.who: Any business or individual with a web project or question
deals.how: Visit dreamsitedesign.com and book a consultation using code VERIFREEDREAM
deals.worth: Web design consultations typically run $75–200/half hour. This one is $0 with the code — a genuine front-door offer from the studio.
deals.caveat: Disclosure: DreamSite Design and Verified Free share an owner. Same scrutiny as every other deal on this page — the consultation is genuinely free, the code is real, and if the studio ever stops honoring it, this listing comes down. The consultation is advice, not deliverables; hired work is quoted separately.

deals.name: The Library Card Stack
deals.type: One card unlocks ~$800+/year in free services
deals.summary: A single public library card — free to get — unlocks Libby (bestseller ebooks and audiobooks), Hoopla (movies, music, comics), Kanopy (art-house films), and often LinkedIn Learning, PressReader (newspapers), and Ancestry Library Edition. Combined value: $800+ per year in subscriptions replaced.
deals.who: Anyone with a residential address — most libraries issue cards for free to residents
deals.how: Walk into your local public library with ID showing your address. Many now offer instant digital cards online too.
deals.worth: Libby replaces Kindle Unlimited (~$144/yr) and Audible (~$180/yr). Hoopla replaces multiple streaming services. Kanopy replaces art-house subscriptions (~$120/yr). LinkedIn Learning is $360/yr. Total: $800+ in services, all on one free card.
deals.caveat: Not every library carries every service — check your library's digital resources page. Multiple library cards (home, work city, non-resident cards) multiply access. This is the single highest-value free thing in America and almost nobody uses it fully.

deals.name: Walmart+ 30-Day Trial (includes Paramount+ and Peacock)
deals.summary: Walmart+ includes Paramount+ and Peacock as bundled perks. The 30-day free trial of Walmart+ gives you both streaming services plus free delivery, gas discounts, and more — all in one trial.
deals.who: New Walmart+ subscribers (US)
deals.how: Sign up at walmart.com/plus — card required, auto-bills at $12.95/month after 30 days
deals.worth: Paramount+ ($7.99/mo) + Peacock ($10.99/mo) + free delivery + gas savings, all bundled into one $12.95/mo subscription — or free for 30 days during the trial.
deals.caveat: Card required, auto-renews. Same rules as every trial on this site: calendar day 27, cancel on the website, screenshot confirmation. The streaming perks are the ad-supported tiers.

deals.name: Hulu 30-Day Free Trial
deals.type: The longest premium streaming trial still standing
deals.summary: Hulu still offers a full 30-day free trial — the longest among major streamers now that Netflix, Disney+, and Max have dropped theirs. That's enough to binge two or three complete series.
deals.who: New Hulu subscribers (US)
deals.how: Sign up at hulu.com — card required, rolls into $9.99/month with ads after 30 days
deals.worth: A full month of a $10/month service: originals, next-day network TV, and a deep back catalog. Time it around a series premiere for maximum value.
deals.caveat: Card required, auto-bills. The 30-day window is generous — plan your watchlist before starting. Cancel the same day you sign up; Hulu keeps the trial running through the full 30 days.

deals.name: Apple TV+ 3 Months Free (with any new Apple device)
deals.summary: Buy any new iPhone, iPad, Mac, or Apple TV and get three months of Apple TV+ free — the same service that runs $12.99/month normally. Activate within 90 days of device setup.
deals.who: Buyers of any new Apple device
deals.how: Open the Apple TV app on your new device within 90 days of first setup — the offer appears automatically
deals.worth: ~$39 in Apple TV+ access. Quality over quantity: Ted Lasso, Severance, The Morning Show, For All Mankind.
deals.caveat: Auto-renews at $12.99/month after the three months. The offer only appears once per device, and only on the new hardware — not on your old iPhone after buying AirPods. Set the cancellation reminder.

deals.name: Anthropic API $5 Free Credit
deals.type: Free developer credits — no card needed
deals.summary: Every new Anthropic API account gets $5 in free credits — no payment details required. Enough to build a small prototype, run a personal automation, or test Claude in your own code.
deals.who: Developers and builders — anyone who can write or paste code
deals.how: Create an account at console.anthropic.com — credits appear automatically, no card needed
deals.worth: $5 covers roughly 1.5 million tokens on the fastest model or ~300K tokens on the mid-tier — enough for a working prototype.
deals.caveat: Developer-facing — this is API access, not the chat interface (which has its own free tier). Credits expire after a period. Once spent, you add a card to continue.

deals.name: ChatGPT Plus Free for US Military & Veterans
deals.type: One year of ChatGPT Plus — free, verified
deals.summary: Active US military servicemembers and veterans get a full year of ChatGPT Plus ($20/month value) free through SheerID verification. No card required for the free year.
deals.who: US active-duty military, reserves, and veterans
deals.how: Verify military status through SheerID on OpenAI's website
deals.worth: $240/year — the full Plus subscription with priority access, the strongest models, and all premium features.
deals.caveat: One year only; renews at full price afterward unless cancelled. Verification through SheerID — have your military documentation ready.

deals.name: Sling Freestream — Free Live TV
deals.type: Permanently free live TV — no card, no trial
deals.summary: Sling Freestream offers ~100 free live channels — mostly news (ABC News, CBS News, BBC) plus some movies and entertainment — with no account, no card, and no trial clock. Separate from Sling's paid plans.
deals.who: Anyone in the US
deals.how: Visit sling.com/freestream or open the Sling app — no signup required
deals.worth: Free live news and entertainment channels at $0 — add it to your Tubi/Pluto/Roku Channel rotation.
deals.caveat: Heavy on news, light on entertainment compared to Tubi or Pluto TV. The paid Sling plans ($40+/month) are a separate product that includes sports and full cable networks.

deals.name: Amazon Trade-In (gift cards for old devices)
deals.type: Free Amazon credit for old electronics
deals.summary: Amazon accepts old Kindles, Echo devices, tablets, and some electronics in any condition — even broken — and gives you an Amazon gift card plus a discount on a replacement device. Not technically free, but turns e-waste into credit.
deals.who: Anyone with old Amazon devices or eligible electronics
deals.how: Visit amazon.com/trade-in, select your device, get a prepaid shipping label, and send it in
deals.worth: Varies: a working Kindle can return $5–25 in credit; broken devices still earn something. The replacement discount (often 20% off) stacks with the credit.
deals.caveat: Values are low for old or broken devices — this is recycling with a small reward, not a resale market. Gift card credit only, not cash.

deals.name: Costco — Risk-Free 100% Satisfaction Guarantee
deals.summary: Costco refunds almost anything, at almost any time, if you're genuinely not satisfied — no receipt needed, since purchases are tied to your membership. The rare exceptions: electronics carry a 90-day window, and items like tires or batteries fall under product warranties. This is a real safety net for honest buyers, not a loophole to exploit.
deals.who: Costco members (membership itself is also refundable if you're unhappy)
deals.how: Bring the item to any Costco warehouse, or start an online return for Costco.com orders — your membership pulls up the purchase
deals.worth: Genuine protection on big-ticket buys: if an appliance disappoints in month four, you're covered where most retailers would refuse.
deals.caveat: Good-faith policy. Costco does track returns, and abuse (habitual 'used it up, brought it back') can get a membership revoked. It works because most people use it honestly — treat it as a satisfaction guarantee, not free rental.

deals.name: REI — 100% Satisfaction Guarantee (1 Year for Co-op Members)
deals.summary: REI Co-op members get a full year to return most gear if it doesn't perform — you can genuinely test hiking boots or a tent and bring them back if they blister or leak. Non-members get 90 days. Electronics are capped at 90 days for everyone. The one-time $30 lifetime membership pays for itself on a single high-ticket return.
deals.who: Anyone; Co-op members ($30 once, for life) get the full year
deals.how: Return in-store free, or by mail for a $5.99 fee ($21.95 oversize) deducted from your refund. Used gear must be cleaned first or it's rejected.
deals.worth: The year-long window on tested outdoor gear is genuinely rare — real protection against gear that fails on the trail.
deals.caveat: The famous 'lifetime' policy is gone (ended 2013 after abuse). Clean your gear before returning — muddy boots get sent back at your expense. This is for gear that genuinely underperforms, not buyer's remorse on a used season.

deals.name: Zappos — 365-Day Free Returns
deals.type: Full year, free return shipping both ways
deals.summary: Zappos gives you a full 365 days to return unworn shoes for a complete refund, and pays return shipping both ways — a big reason it became the default for buying shoes online, where fit is a gamble. Shoes must be unworn and in the original box with tags (and security tag, if one came attached).
deals.who: Anyone shopping Zappos.com
deals.how: Start a return online within 365 days; Zappos emails a prepaid label and credits your original payment in 5–10 business days
deals.worth: The year window plus free two-way shipping removes the risk from buying shoes you can't try on first.
deals.caveat: Unworn only — try shoes on carpet indoors, not outside. This protects honest 'didn't fit / didn't suit me' returns; wearing them out and returning them is return fraud, which raises prices for everyone. Note the discount outlet (6pm) does NOT share this policy.

deals.name: Target — 90 Days (1 Year on Target-Owned Brands)
deals.summary: Target gives 90 days on most items with a receipt, and a full year on its own brands — Cat & Jack kids' clothing, Good & Gather, Boots & Barkley and others — even after they're worn or washed. RedCard holders get an extra 30 days on the standard window. Cat & Jack's year-long guarantee on worn-through kids' clothes is legitimately generous.
deals.who: Anyone; RedCard holders get +30 days on standard returns
deals.how: Return in-store with receipt (or via Target Circle app / Drive Up at many locations); online orders include free return shipping
deals.worth: The Target-brand year — especially Cat & Jack for kids who destroy clothes — is real, honest value for families.
deals.caveat: The one-year window is for Target-owned brands only, not everything in the store. Keep the receipt or use a tracked account. Designed for genuine wear-and-fail, not wardrobing.

deals.name: Patagonia — Ironclad Guarantee (repair first)
deals.type: Satisfaction guarantee with free repair for wear
deals.summary: Patagonia stands behind everything it makes: if an item doesn't meet your needs, return it for a repair, replacement, or refund. What sets it apart is that normal wear-and-tear damage is repaired — often free or at low cost — rather than tossed, in keeping with the company's reduce-waste mission. It's a guarantee built to extend a product's life, not to churn it.
deals.who: Anyone who buys Patagonia
deals.how: Return unsatisfactory items for refund/replacement; send worn gear to Patagonia's repair service (the largest garment repair operation in North America)
deals.worth: Free or cheap repairs on gear you intend to keep for years — value measured in decades, not a return window.
deals.caveat: This one actively rewards good faith: the ethos is 'keep it longer,' not 'return it faster.' Using the repair service as intended is exactly the point — no misuse angle here, just a company that would rather fix than replace.

comparisons.title: Best Free Image Editors Compared
comparisons.desc: Photopea vs GIMP vs Canva — which free image editor actually fits your workflow? Side-by-side scores, features, and honest picks.
comparisons.intro: Three genuinely free image editors, three different trade-offs. Here's what each one actually gives you — and which one fits.
comparisons.verdict: For quick web graphics: Canva. For real photo editing without installing anything: Photopea. For the full Photoshop-class toolset you own forever: GIMP. All three are genuinely free — the choice is about workflow, not price.

comparisons.title: Best Free Video Editors Compared
comparisons.desc: DaVinci Resolve vs Blender vs OBS — the free video tools professionals actually use, compared honestly.
comparisons.intro: These aren't toy editors with watermarks — they're the tools used on real productions. Here's how they split the work.
comparisons.verdict: For video editing and color grading: DaVinci Resolve, no contest — it's the industry standard, free. For 3D animation and VFX: Blender. For live streaming and screen recording: OBS. They complement more than they compete.

comparisons.title: Best Free Streaming Services Compared
comparisons.desc: Tubi vs Pluto TV vs Kanopy vs Hoopla vs The Roku Channel — every genuinely free streaming option, ranked.
comparisons.intro: Five ways to watch movies and shows without a subscription or a credit card. Each one earns its free differently.
comparisons.verdict: For the biggest free catalog: Tubi. For channel-surfing like cable: Pluto TV. For art-house and documentaries ad-free: Kanopy (library card). For everything in one app — movies, audiobooks, comics: Hoopla (library card). Use all five; they're all free and they barely overlap.

comparisons.title: Free Tax Filing Compared
comparisons.desc: TurboTax Free vs FreeTaxUSA vs H&R Block Free vs Cash App Taxes — which one is actually free for YOUR return?
comparisons.intro: Four 'free' tax filing options with very different definitions of the word. Here's who qualifies, who gets squeezed, and who files every return free.
comparisons.verdict: FreeTaxUSA wins: free federal filing for every complexity level, flat $15 for state, no bait-and-switch. Cash App Taxes covers most returns completely free. TurboTax and H&R Block are free only for the simplest returns — and both are designed to upgrade you mid-flow.

comparisons.title: Free Password Managers Compared
comparisons.desc: Bitwarden vs LastPass free — one open-source champion vs one cautionary tale. The honest comparison.
comparisons.intro: Two free password managers with very different reputations. One syncs everywhere and was independently audited; the other restricts you to one device type and suffered a major breach.
comparisons.verdict: Bitwarden. It's not close. Free Bitwarden syncs across all devices, is open source, independently audited, and has never been breached. Free LastPass locks you to one device type and carries the weight of 2022. Switch today; the import takes minutes.

comparisons.title: Free VPNs Compared — The Honest Guide
comparisons.desc: Proton VPN (genuinely free) vs Hola (fake free) vs NordVPN (not free at all). The only VPN comparison that asks who's paying.
comparisons.intro: The question with free VPNs is never 'does it work' — it's 'who's paying for the servers.' Three answers, three very different verdicts.
comparisons.verdict: Proton VPN is the only honest answer: genuinely free, no data cap, funded by paid subscribers, independently audited. Hola historically monetized your bandwidth. NordVPN isn't free at all. For a free VPN, there is one choice.

comparisons.title: Free Office Suites Compared
comparisons.desc: LibreOffice vs Google Docs vs Microsoft 365 Online — three ways to write, calculate, and present for $0.
comparisons.intro: A desktop app you own, a cloud suite tied to Google, and Microsoft's own web apps — all free, all real, all different.
comparisons.verdict: For offline power with no account: LibreOffice. For collaboration and cloud-first workflow: Google. For Microsoft compatibility in a browser: Microsoft 365 Online. Most people should start with Google (already have the account) and reach for LibreOffice when they need offline or privacy.

comparisons.title: Free Messaging Apps Compared
comparisons.desc: Signal vs WhatsApp vs Telegram — all free, very different privacy models. Which one deserves your conversations?
comparisons.intro: Three billion-user messaging apps, all free, with encryption promises that mean very different things. The price is $0 everywhere; the privacy isn't.
comparisons.verdict: For privacy: Signal — end-to-end encrypted, nonprofit, no metadata collection. For reach: WhatsApp — three billion users, encrypted content but Meta collects metadata. For features and large groups: Telegram — but regular chats are NOT end-to-end encrypted by default. Know the trade-off you're making.

comparisons.title: Best Free Learning Platforms Compared
comparisons.desc: Khan Academy vs freeCodeCamp vs MIT OCW vs CS50 — the truly free education that rivals paid courses.
comparisons.intro: Four platforms where 'free' means actually free — no hidden tier, no audit-mode trick, no card. Here's what each one teaches best.
comparisons.verdict: For K–12 through college fundamentals: Khan Academy. For learning to code from zero to employable: freeCodeCamp. For university-depth self-study in any field: MIT OCW. For the single best intro to computer science: CS50. All are truly free. Use them together.

comparisons.title: Free Website Hosting Compared
comparisons.desc: Netlify vs GitHub vs Wix vs WordPress.com — which free hosting actually lets you build without a banner or a clock?
comparisons.intro: Four ways to put a website online for $0 — with very different definitions of 'yours.'
comparisons.verdict: For a clean site you fully own: Netlify (custom domain, no banner, drag-and-drop deploy). For developer projects and portfolios: GitHub Pages. For a drag-and-drop builder you don't mind wearing a banner: Wix. WordPress.com's free tier is the most restricted. Netlify + a $12 domain is the best free stack.

changelog.change: DOJ settlement — $150M over cancellation dark patterns
changelog.detail: Adobe settled a Department of Justice lawsuit over hidden early-termination fees and obstructive cancellation flows: $75 million in free services to affected customers, $75 million to the DOJ. Customers charged the 50% exit fee between January 2019 and July 2025 may be eligible.
Ad-supported free streaming shut down December 31, 2025. Every plan now requires payment. Some older titles still surface on Tubi and Pluto TV.

changelog.change: Energy meter replaces hearts on mobile
changelog.detail: The new energy system drains even on correct answers, capping most free mobile users at a couple of lessons per day. Web version still runs the gentler hearts system.
Down from 2,000 contacts in 2021, then 500, now 250 with a 500-send monthly cap. Fourth cut since the Intuit acquisition.
One-on-one calls were previously unlimited on the free plan. Now all meetings hosted by free accounts cap at 40 minutes.
The Federal Trade Commission found Intuit's 'free' advertising deceptive — most filers who start the 'free' flow are forced into paid tiers mid-return.
Even paying Prime members now see ads unless they pay an additional ad-free upcharge — a second toll booth on a service already behind a paywall.
The ~25-generation free trial was suspended due to abuse and demand. As of July 2026 it has never returned to the main platform.

changelog.change: Free restricted to one device type
changelog.detail: Free users can now sync passwords on mobile OR desktop, not both — crippling the core purpose of a password manager. Drove mass migration to Bitwarden.
Attackers accessed encrypted password vaults. LastPass says strong master passwords protect users; security researchers noted weaker defaults on older accounts.
Intuit withdrew from the IRS Free File partnership that required genuine free filing for low-income taxpayers, making TurboTax's 'free' its own definition rather than the IRS's.
